title: Secure Media Destruction Workflow Completion for Backup Tapes with CHD

compliance.assurance_rationale: PCI DSS 3.2.1 — Reviewed: SPL provides contributing evidence for this regulatory requirement. The search query directly addresses the monitoring objective specified by the clause.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that PCI DSS 3.2.1 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.11.58: Secure Media Destruction Workflow Completion for Backup Tapes with CHD.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.11.58' running on index=logistics sourcetype="media:chain" — barcode, status, destroyed_time, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

spl:
index=logistics sourcetype="media:chain" earliest=-90d
| where classification="PCI_CHD"
| where match(status,"(?i)in_transit|awaiting_destroy|stored")
| eval age_days=round((now()-created_time)/86400,1)
| where age_days>120
| stats max(age_days) as oldest_days by barcode, status, vault_site
| sort - oldest_days
description: Tracks chain-of-custody and destruction certificates for media classified as containing account data so classified media is destroyed when no longer needed for business or legal reasons.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for PCI DSS compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes logistics and sourcetypes media:chain. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Ingest vendor destruction webhook as status `destroyed`. (2) Align retention to legal hold exceptions via lookup. (3) Escalate aged media weekly. (4) Attach certificates to GRC record. Indexes required: `index=logistics`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=media:chain`.
visualization: Table (oldest media), gauge (avg age_days), timeline (status transitions).
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (1928) or `sourcetype="ironmountain:destruction"`.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=logistics` `sourcetype="media:chain"` — `barcode`, `status`, `destroyed_time`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Ingest vendor destruction webhook as status `destroyed`. (2) Align retention to legal hold exceptions via lookup. (3) Escalate aged media weekly. (4) Attach certificates to GRC record.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=logistics sourcetype="media:chain" earliest=-90d
| where classification="PCI_CHD"
| where match(status,"(?i)in_transit|awaiting_destroy|stored")
| eval age_days=round((now()-created_time)/86400,1)
| where age_days>120
| stats max(age_days) as oldest_days by barcode, status, vault_site
| sort - oldest_days
```

Understanding this SPL

**Secure Media Destruction Workflow Completion for Backup Tapes with CHD (PCI DSS Req 9.5.1, 3.2.1)** — Tracks chain-of-custody and destruction certificates for media classified as containing account data so classified media is destroyed when no longer needed for business or legal reasons.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=logistics` `sourcetype="media:chain"` — `barcode`, `status`, `destroyed_time`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (1928) or `sourcetype="ironmountain:destruction"`. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: logistics; **sourcetype**: media:chain. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=logistics, sourcetype="media:chain", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Filters the current rows with `where classification="PCI_CHD"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Filters the current rows with `where match(status,"(?i)in_transit|awaiting_destroy|stored")` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **age_days** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where age_days>120` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by barcode, status, vault_site** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (oldest media), gauge (avg age_days), timeline (status transitions).
grandmaExplanation: We watch for tracks chain-of-custody and destruction certificates for media classified as containing account data so classified media is destroyed when no longer needed for business or legal reasons. This matters because it catches problems that could lead to audit findings or compliance gaps.
